title: 'Feeding illusion or ending it' — Sep 2019 family-conflict decision note
html:
TL;DR

  • Six-line decision-mode note from Sep 25, 2019. Title is the binary choice — "Feeding illusion or ending it". The six lines walk through a household-tension situation that's escalating: "Is the move to move out / Will that teach him a lesson / Tell him that I talked to Travis / Shit is kind of out of control / Focus on shit we can control / Not. To depend on other people."
  • Yahir at 16, deliberating a "move out" decision over a him-pronouned conflict (most likely his father, given the "Give your dad an inch he will take a mile" line he writes 4 weeks later in 2019 10 22 the smartest nigga isnt the smartest nigga in the room the s and the recurring household-tension thread through 2020-2024).
  • This is the genesis source for the travis entity stub — the line "Tell him that I talked to Travis" uses Travis as a back-channel / authority-leverage in the family conflict. Travis is Yahir's outside-adult go-to in the moment.
  • The thinking-form is the two-column Stoic split — "Focus on shit we can control / Not. to depend on other people" — Marcus Aurelius dichotomy of control, deployed by a 16-year-old in a real-time household crisis. Pair against 2019 08 12 keep things simple (the philosophy scratchpad from 6 weeks earlier) — that's where he was learning the frame; this note is where he uses it.
  • "Feeding illusion or ending it" — title-as-thesis. The illusion isn't named, but the framing is: any further accommodation is feeding something he no longer believes is true. This is the earliest extant specimen of Yahir naming a relational dynamic as an illusion to be ended, a verb-shape that recurs across the 2024-2025 Sam arc and the 2025-2026 Emily questioning notes.

Key quotes

"Is the move to move out / Will that teach him a lesson"

"Tell him that I talked to Travis"

"Focus on shit we can control / Not. to depend on other people"

Why it matters in the graph

  • The first end-the-illusion specimen in the corpus — same verb-shape Yahir uses 5+ years later when ending the sam relationship and questioning the Emily alignment. The instinct is older than the relationships it later names.
  • Establishes travis as a 16-year-old's outside-adult lifeline during family conflict — one of the named teen-era humans (alongside Brenda, mrs bland, scott carnes) who scaffolded Yahir through the grants pass teen era.
  • The dichotomy-of-control deployment here is what makes the 2020 "Opportunity is anywhere and everywhere" line in 2020 03 30 facebook pandemic watchlist readable as character-already-formed rather than as pandemic-induced philosophizing.

Open questions

  • Did Yahir actually move out, or did the conflict de-escalate?
  • The "him" is unnamed — confirm via 2019-2020 cross-references whether this is Dad, brother, or someone else.
  • What did Travis advise? The corpus doesn't yet hold the conversation contents.

Privacy redaction

  • The conflict-target is not named in the source — no redaction needed. Travis is named (already a documented stub).

Wikilinks

travis · yahir family · grants pass teen era · lineage as fate · fear of not doing · the emily arc · sam
